Adapter.description: Upstream API protocol family used for provider dispatch.

allowed_models.description: Model identifiers this key may use. An empty array denies access to every model.

key_hash.description: SHA-256 hexadecimal hash of the plaintext bearer. The proxy hashes incoming bearer tokens before lookup.

rate_limit.description: Request, token, and concurrency limits for this key.

team_id.description: Team this API key belongs to. Used for matching team-scope rate limit policies.

user_id.description: Org member who owns this key. Used for matching member-scope rate limit policies.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

value.description: Stored caller API key configuration with plaintext credentials redacted.

ApiKeyRequest.description: Self-hosted Admin API request body for creating or updating a caller API key. Cloud-projected ownership fields are not accepted by this API.

allowed_models.description: Model aliases this caller API key may use.

key_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the caller-facing plaintext key.

rate_limit.description: Request, token, and concurrency limits for this key.

entry.description: Updated caller API key resource with the new key hash stored and plaintext credentials redacted.

plaintext.description: New plaintext bearer token. Returned only once after rotation.

enabled.description: Whether background health checks are enabled for this model.

ignore_statuses.description: Upstream status codes to ignore when evaluating background check failures.

interval_seconds.description: Seconds between background health checks. Minimum: 5.

max_tokens.description: Maximum completion tokens requested during each background health check.

prompt.description: Prompt sent to the model during each background health check.

stale_after_seconds.description: Seconds after which the last completed background check is considered stale.

timeout_seconds.description: Request timeout in seconds for each background health check. Minimum: 1.

BedrockAWSCredentials.description: AWS credentials for a Bedrock guardrail.

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID for static Bedrock guardrail credentials.

kind.description: Credential mode. Use `static` for explicitly configured AWS access keys.

secret_access_key.description: Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged. The data plane passes it to the AWS SDK's static credentials provider.

BedrockLatencyMode.description: Per-guardrail latency policy for `kind: "bedrock"`. `serial` waits for the guardrail response. `timed` aborts at `timeout_ms` and applies `fail_open`.

kind.description: Latency mode. Use `serial` to wait for the Bedrock guardrail response.
Latency mode. Use `timed` to stop waiting after `timeout_ms`.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the Bedrock guardrail response.

CacheBackend.description: Cache backend choice for requests matched by a cache policy. `redis` requires `cache.redis`. Otherwise matching requests are not cached.

CachePolicy.description: Semantic cache policy for chat requests.

backend.description: Cache backend used for matching requests.

enabled.description: When false, the cache gate skips this policy. Allows operators to stage a rule before enabling it.

name.description: Operator-facing name that surfaces in metric labels and cache headers.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

snapshot_age_seconds.description: Seconds since the watch supervisor last applied a snapshot, or null before the first apply.

snapshot_revision.description: Highest configuration-store revision reflected in the current snapshot.

CooldownConfig.description: Request-path cooldown settings for a direct model after retryable upstream failures.

default_seconds.description: Cooldown TTL in seconds when the upstream did not supply a `Retry-After` header or `honor_retry_after` is `false`.

enabled.description: Whether cooldown is active for this model. Set to `false` to keep the model in rotation regardless of upstream failures.

honor_retry_after.description: Whether to use the upstream's `Retry-After` header as the cooldown TTL when it contains seconds.

max_seconds.description: Upper bound on cooldown TTL when `Retry-After` is used.

trigger_on_transport.description: Whether transport, decode, or stream-abort errors trigger cooldown.

trigger_statuses.description: Status codes that trigger cooldown, covering authentication failures, rate limits, and transient server errors. Caller-side validation errors such as `400`, `403`, and `422` are excluded.

deleted.description: Always `true` when the resource was deleted.

judge.description: Direct model that combines successful panel responses.

min_responses.description: Minimum successful panel responses required before judge synthesis. When omitted, the gateway requires the smaller of 2 and the panel size.

panel.description: Direct models called concurrently for each ensemble request.

timeout_ms.description: Per-call upstream deadline applied to each panel member and the judge. Set `0` or omit it to disable the ensemble-level deadline.

Guardrail.description: Content policy evaluated before or after upstream calls.

oneOf.description: In-process literal/regex blocklist. Always available.

kind.description: Guardrail provider type. Use `keyword` for literal and regular expression matching.

patterns.description: Blocklist patterns. An empty list is valid and allows every request, equivalent to `enabled: false`.

oneOf.title: Keyword

oneOf.description: AWS Bedrock managed guardrail using `ApplyGuardrail` on input, output, or both.

guardrail_id.description: Guardrail identifier issued by the AWS console.

kind.description: Guardrail provider type. Use `bedrock` for Amazon Bedrock Guardrails.

latency_mode.description: Bedrock guardrail latency policy. Use `timed` with `timeout_ms` to cap wait time.

region.description: AWS region for the Bedrock endpoint, such as `us-east-1`.

oneOf.title: AWS Bedrock

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shield. Detects jailbreak and indirect injection attacks via the `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt` API.

api_key.description: Azure subscription key sent with the `Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` header. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint, e.g. `https://my-resource.cognitiveservices.azure.com`. The data plane appends `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt?api-version=2024-09-01`.

kind.description: Guardrail provider type. Use `azure_content_safety` for Azure Prompt Shield.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

oneOf.title: Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shield

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Text Moderation. Category-severity and blocklist moderation via the `/contentsafety/text:analyze` API, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

api_key.description: Azure subscription key sent with the `Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` header. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

blocklist_names.description: Azure CS blocklist names to match against.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint. The data plane appends `/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2024-09-01`.

kind.description: Guardrail provider type. Use `azure_content_safety_text_moderation` for Azure text moderation.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to allow output when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled, an Azure outage does not release unscanned model output.

output_type.description: Severity scale. Use `FourSeverityLevels` for 0, 2, 4, and 6, or `EightSeverityLevels` for 0 through 7.

severity_threshold.description: General severity threshold. A category at or above it blocks.

severity_threshold_by_category.description: Per-category threshold overrides. These take precedence over the general threshold.

stream_processing_mode.description: `window` for sliding-window incremental release or `buffer_full` for whole-response hold-back.

text_source.description: Input-hook text selection. Use `concatenate_all_content` to include all message content. Ignored on the output hook.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in characters for window mode.

oneOf.title: Azure AI Content Safety Text Moderation

oneOf.description: Aliyun content-safety guardrail. Risk-level moderation via the `TextModerationPlus` action on `green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

access_key_secret.description: Aliyun AccessKey secret. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged. Used to sign the request.

endpoint.description: Explicit endpoint override as a full URL with no trailing slash. When set, it takes precedence over `region`.

kind.description: Guardrail provider type. Use `aliyun_text_moderation` for Aliyun text moderation.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to allow output when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled, an Aliyun outage does not release unscanned model output.

region.description: Aliyun region the guardrail lives in, e.g. `cn-shanghai`. The data plane builds the endpoint `https://green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`.

risk_level_threshold.description: Minimum risk level that triggers a block: `low`, `medium`, or `high`. A returned level at or above this blocks.

stream_processing_mode.description: `window` for sliding-window incremental release or `buffer_full` for whole-response hold-back.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in characters when window mode is used. Aliyun limits each `llm_response_moderation` call to 2,000 characters.

oneOf.title: Aliyun Text Moderation

created_at.description: RFC3339 creation timestamp. When present, guardrails are evaluated from oldest to newest. Resources without this timestamp sort after resources that have it.

direction.description: Attachment direction hint: `input`, `output`, or `both`. Stored for compatibility. Current hook selection still follows `hook_point`.

enabled.description: When false, the chain skips this rule entirely. Allows operators to stage a rule before enabling it.

enforcement_mode.description: How the data plane behaves when this guardrail fires. `monitor` is stored for compatibility but not yet enforced.

fail_open.description: Behavior when a remote API guardrail cannot reach its upstream. `true` allows the request and records the bypass reason in `usage_events.guardrail_bypassed_reason`. `false` blocks with 422. Keyword guardrails do not use this setting.

hook_point.description: Where in the lifecycle this rule runs.

mandatory.description: Whether guardrail evaluation errors should be fatal. Stored for compatibility. Current enforcement still follows `fail_open`.

name.description: Operator-facing name that surfaces in metric labels and error reasons.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

GuardrailHookPoint.description: What part of the request lifecycle a guardrail inspects.

oneOf.description: Run on the request payload before the upstream call.
oneOf.title: Input

oneOf.description: Run on the upstream response before the cache write + render.
oneOf.title: Output

oneOf.description: Run on both input and output.
oneOf.title: Both

config.description: Configuration watch freshness. Omitted when the watch supervisor is not wired into the admin state.

status.description: Fixed success marker for this response. Successful responses currently always return `ok`. This field does not summarize model health. Use `models[].health` and `config` for actionable health details.

Judge.description: The judge model that synthesizes the panel responses into one answer. `model` references a direct model alias.

model.description: Model alias for the direct model that synthesizes panel responses.

synthesis_prompt.description: Override for the built-in synthesis prompt template.

KeywordPattern.description: Literal or regular-expression pattern used by a keyword guardrail.

oneOf.description: Literal string to match.

kind.description: Pattern type. Use `literal` to match the value as plain text.

oneOf.title: Literal

oneOf.description: Regular expression pattern to match.

kind.description: Pattern type. Use `regex` to match the value as a regular expression.

oneOf.title: Regex

allowed_cidrs.description: Client IP allowlist in CIDR notation. Empty or absent allows all clients.

cooldown.description: Direct-model-only request-path cooldown configuration. Omit this field to use the built-in cooldown behavior.

cost.description: Per-token cost for budget tracking. Omit it when cost tracking is not needed.

display_name.description: Operator-facing unique label. Surfaces on `/v1/models`, `req.model` on chat completions, `ApiKey.allowed_models`, and the dashboard model list. `Resource::name()` returns this.

ensemble.description: Ensemble configuration for panel calls and judge synthesis.

model_name.description: Upstream model identifier sent in provider requests. Routing and ensemble models leave this field unset.

provider.description: Upstream vendor identity used for dispatch, compatibility checks, telemetry, and access logs. Routing and ensemble models leave this field unset.

provider_key_id.description: Provider key resource ID used to authenticate upstream requests. Routing and ensemble models leave this field unset.

rate_limit.description: Request, token, and concurrency limits for this model.

routing.description: Virtual routing configuration. When set, the gateway selects a target from `routing.targets` and uses that target model's `provider`, `model_name`, and `provider_key_id` fields for upstream dispatch.

stream_timeout.description: Maximum gap in milliseconds between upstream streaming chunks. `0` or absent falls back to `timeout`.

timeout.description: End-to-end timeout in milliseconds for non-streaming upstream calls. `0` or absent disables the non-streaming timeout.

ModelCost.description: Per-token cost for budget tracking. Both values are in USD per 1,000 tokens.

input_per_1k.description: Prompt token cost in USD per 1,000 tokens.

output_per_1k.description: Completion token cost in USD per 1,000 tokens.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

health.description: Numeric model health level. Lower values indicate healthier states. A value of 0 indicates healthy, 1 indicates degraded, and 2 indicates down.

name.description: Model alias used by proxy callers.

ModelKind.description: Model resource kind. `direct` models dispatch to one upstream target. `routing` models select from configured routing targets.

ModelStatusView.description: Runtime routing status for one model.

cooldown_until.description: Time when the current request-path cooldown expires. Omitted when the model is not in cooldown.

display_name.description: Model alias used by proxy callers.

id.description: Resolved model id. Direct-model runtime status is keyed by this id.

kind.description: Whether the row describes a direct upstream model or a routing model.

last_check_status.description: HTTP status code from the last background model check.

last_checked_at.description: Time of the last background model health check. Omitted before a check has completed.

status.description: Runtime routing status for this model.

status_reason.description: Reason for the current runtime status, when available.

ObjectStoreAuthMode.description: How the data plane obtains credentials for the object-storage bucket.

oneOf.description: Resolve `credential_ref` to static keys from data plane environment variables named `OBJSTORE_CRED_<SLUG>_<FIELD>`.
oneOf.title: Credential reference

oneOf.description: Use the data plane host's attached cloud identity. Supported for S3 and GCS only.
oneOf.title: Cloud identity

oneOf.description: gzip compression as defined by RFC 1952. Accepted by Snowpipe and Auto Loader.
oneOf.title: Gzip

oneOf.description: No compression. Emits raw NDJSON.
oneOf.title: None

ObjectStoreProvider.description: Object-storage backend selector. The sink builds one backend client per variant. Batching, key layout, and retry behavior are shared.

oneOf.description: OTLP/HTTP trace exporter configuration.

content_max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes per captured prompt or response field when `content_mode` is `full`.

content_mode.description: Controls whether spans include prompt and response content. `metadata_only` omits content. `full` includes content truncated by `content_max_bytes`.

endpoint.description: Full URL of the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint. Include the receiver's expected path, such as `/v1/traces`.

headers.description: Static headers attached to every export request, such as authorization or vendor-specific API-key headers.

kind.description: Exporter type. Use `otlp_http` to send telemetry to an OTLP HTTP endpoint.

sample_rate.description: Fraction of requests exported as traces, from `0.0` to `1.0`.

oneOf.title: OTLP HTTP

oneOf.description: Aliyun SLS PutLogs exporter configuration.

content_max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes per captured prompt or response field when `content_mode` is `full`.

content_mode.description: Controls whether logs include prompt and response content. `metadata_only` omits content. `full` includes content truncated by `content_max_bytes`.

credential_ref.description: Credential reference resolved by the data plane at delivery time. The plaintext AccessKey is not stored in this resource.

endpoint.description: SLS regional endpoint host without a scheme, such as `ap-southeast-3.log.aliyuncs.com`. Signed requests are sent to this endpoint with the SLS project as the host prefix.

kind.description: Exporter type. Use `aliyun_sls` to send telemetry to Aliyun SLS.

logstore.description: SLS logstore that receives the request-event logs.

project.description: SLS project that prefixes the regional endpoint in signed requests.

oneOf.title: Aliyun SLS

oneOf.description: Object-storage exporter configuration for S3, GCS, Azure Blob, and compatible S3 backends.

auth_mode.description: How the data plane authenticates to the bucket.

bucket.description: Bucket for S3 or GCS, or container for Azure Blob, that receives exported files.

compression.description: Compression applied to each NDJSON file before upload.

credential_ref.description: Credential reference resolved by the data plane at delivery time. Required when `auth_mode` is `credential_ref`.

endpoint.description: Backend host override for S3-compatible stores such as MinIO, Aliyun OSS, or Cloudflare R2. When omitted, the provider's native endpoint is used.

kind.description: Exporter type. Use `object_store` to write telemetry batches to object storage.

prefix.description: Key prefix the partition path is appended to, e.g. `ai-gateway`. The full key is `<prefix>/org=…/env=…/table=…/dt=…/hh=…/<file>`.

provider.description: Which object-storage backend the bucket lives in.

region.description: AWS region for S3 SigV4 signature scope. Set this for S3 buckets outside `us-east-1`. Ignored for GCS and Azure Blob.

oneOf.title: Object storage

oneOf.description: Datadog native Logs HTTP intake exporter configuration.

content_max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes per captured prompt or response field when `content_mode` is `full`. Keep this under Datadog intake limits to avoid delivery errors.

content_mode.description: Controls whether logs include prompt and response content. `metadata_only` omits content. `full` includes content truncated by `content_max_bytes`.

credential_ref.description: Credential reference resolved by the data plane at delivery time. The plaintext Datadog API key is not stored in this resource.

ddsource.description: Datadog `ddsource` reserved attribute. Identifies the integration or source.

kind.description: Exporter type. Use `datadog` to send telemetry to Datadog Logs intake.

service.description: Datadog `service` reserved attribute. Every log from this exporter is tagged with this service name in Datadog Log Explorer.

tags.description: Operator-defined tags rendered into Datadog's comma-joined `ddtags` reserved attribute. For example, `["team:platform", "tier:prod"]` becomes `team:platform,tier:prod`. Leave empty when no tags should be sent.

oneOf.title: Datadog

enabled.description: Whether this exporter is active. Disabled exporters remain configured but do not receive telemetry.

name.description: Operator-facing label, surfaced in logs and dashboard lists. The etcd key UUID is the resource identity.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

OnAllFilteredPolicy.description: Behavior when every routing target is filtered out by runtime health or cooldown state.

oneOf.description: Return `503` with a fixed `Retry-After` hint.
oneOf.title: Fail

oneOf.description: Route to the original candidate list in declaration order even when all targets were filtered by health or cooldown status. Use only when maintaining availability is preferred over avoiding recently unhealthy targets.
oneOf.title: Original order

PanelMember.description: One member of an ensemble panel. `model` references a direct model alias.

model.description: Model alias for a direct model that receives one panel request.

seed.description: Sampling seed for this panel member.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for this panel member. Omit it to keep the request's temperature.

weight.description: Reserved for the future voting/quorum strategy. Ignored by the v1 synthesis path.

ParamConstraints.description: Numeric range clamps applied to chat-completion request bodies.

temperature_max.description: Upper bound for `temperature`. Values above this are clamped to this value. If omitted, no upper bound is applied.

temperature_min.description: Lower bound for `temperature`. Values below this are clamped to this value. If omitted, no lower bound is applied.

adapter.description: Upstream API protocol family used when provider-specific dispatch is unavailable.

api_base.description: Override base URL for the upstream provider. Required for custom or OpenAI-compatible providers that should not use a built-in vendor endpoint.

display_name.description: Operator-facing label, unique within the gateway. Surfaces in the Admin API list view and in dashboard UIs that wrap this resource.

provider.description: Upstream provider identifier, such as `"deepseek"`, `"openai"`, or a model catalog ID. The gateway uses this value for provider-specific dispatch and base URL validation.

request.description: Per-key request-shape overrides applied by supported provider paths before dispatch to the upstream provider.

response.description: Per-key response-shape overrides applied by provider bridges that support response transformation.

secret.description: Upstream provider's API key. The data plane receives plaintext so it can authenticate to the upstream provider. Protect the configuration store and transport accordingly.

strip_headers.description: Inbound headers removed before passthrough forwarding.

telemetry_tags.description: Telemetry tags carried alongside the key for metric and log emission.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

allowed_models.description: Model aliases this caller API key may use.

key_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the caller-facing plaintext key.

rate_limit.description: Request, token, and concurrency limits for this key.

id.description: Resource ID generated by the Admin API.

revision.description: Monotonic resource revision. Create operations start at 1, and update or rotate operations increment the revision.

value.description: Stored caller API key configuration with plaintext credentials redacted.

rph.description: Requests per 3,600-second window. There is no per-hour token limit field.

rps.description: Requests per 1-second window. There is no per-second token limit field.

RequestOverrides.description: Per-`ProviderKey` request-shape overrides. Use these fields to rename request body parameters, clamp supported numeric parameters, add fallback outbound headers, or add fallback outbound body fields.

default_body_fields.description: `apply_default_body_fields` input. Top-level body fields added when the caller did not set them. `serde_json::Map` preserves insertion order on serialize, matching the etcd round-trip.

default_headers.description: `apply_default_headers` input. Top-level headers added to the outbound request when the caller did not set them. Reserved auth headers are dropped by `apply_default_headers` as defense-in-depth.

param_constraints.description: Parameter constraints applied to the outbound request. If omitted, no clamping is applied.

param_renames.description: `apply_param_renames` input. Top-level body keys named on the left are renamed to the right. Leave empty to preserve request parameter names.

ResponseOverrides.description: Per-`ProviderKey` response-shape overrides. Use these fields to describe stream termination behavior, flatten list-style content when needed, select an error envelope strategy, or lift provider-specific reasoning content.

content_list_to_string.description: When `true`, the request-body `messages[*].content` array of text blocks gets flattened to a single string before dispatch.

error_envelope.description: Stored error-envelope preference for compatibility with control-plane configuration. The proxy does not currently apply this field.

reasoning_field.description: Path used to extract reasoning content from the provider response. If omitted or empty, no reasoning field is lifted. Example: `"delta.reasoning_content"`.

stream_done_marker.description: Stream `[DONE]` terminator expectation. If omitted, either presence or absence of the terminator is accepted.

max_fallbacks.description: Max number of later targets to attempt after the initial target fails permanently. When omitted, all later targets may be attempted.

on_all_filtered.description: Policy to apply when runtime status filtering removes every candidate.

retries.description: Retry attempts on the current target before failing over.

retry_on_429.description: Whether upstream 429 participates in retries and failover.

strategy.description: Strategy used to select a target for each request.

targets.description: Ordered set of direct models available to this routing model.

oneOf.description: Cycle through targets in declaration order.
oneOf.title: Round robin

oneOf.description: Pick targets by configured weight. Missing target weights fall back to 1.
oneOf.title: Weighted

oneOf.description: Always start with the first target and move to later targets only after failure.
oneOf.title: Failover

RoutingTarget.description: One destination in a routing configuration. `model` references a direct model alias.

model.description: Model alias for a direct model that can receive routed traffic.

weight.description: Target weight for `weighted` routing. Other strategies ignore this field.

RuntimeStatus.description: `healthy` indicates that the model can receive traffic. `unhealthy` indicates that recent background checks failed and routing should avoid the model. `cooldown` indicates that recent request-path failures temporarily exclude the model until `cooldown_until`. `not_applicable` is used for routing models because runtime checks apply to their direct targets.

SlsContentMode.description: Content-capture mode for an observability exporter.

oneOf.description: Operational metadata only. Prompt and response content are omitted.
oneOf.title: Metadata only

oneOf.description: Metadata plus the captured request prompt and assembled response.
oneOf.title: Full content

StreamDoneMarker.description: Stream `[DONE]` terminator policy for an SSE response. Values are `"required"`, `"optional"`, or `"none"`.

oneOf.description: Upstream is expected to emit `data: [DONE]`. Absence is logged as a diagnostic warning.
oneOf.title: Required

oneOf.description: Either presence or absence is acceptable. Used when the upstream is OpenAI-compatible but does not require the terminator.
oneOf.title: Optional

oneOf.description: Upstream is expected to omit the marker and terminate on connection close.
oneOf.title: None

nanos_since_epoch.description: Nanosecond offset within the current second.

secs_since_epoch.description: Whole seconds since the Unix epoch.

TelemetryTags.description: Telemetry attribution tags emitted with requests routed through this provider key.

branded_provider.description: Branded provider slug for catalog entries, such as `"openai"` or `"anthropic"`. Bring-your-own providers leave this field unset.

byo_label.description: Operator-defined label for bring-your-own entries, such as an internal team name. Catalog entries leave this field unset.

featured.description: Whether this provider is surfaced in the featured list.

kind.description: Provider-key category, such as `"catalog"` for curated providers or `"byo"` for bring-your-own providers.

pk_label.description: Operator-defined label for this provider key, such as `"production"` or `"shared-test"`.

ProxyBearer.description: Proxy API key. Used only by `/playground/chat/completions`.

info.description:
The AISIX Admin API is the self-hosted management interface for configuring the gateway at runtime. Use it when you operate AISIX directly and need to create or update models, caller API keys, provider credentials, guardrails, cache policies, and observability exporters.

AISIX Cloud does not expose this listener. Managed configuration is handled by the Cloud control plane.
info.title: AISIX Admin API

get.description: Open the browser UI that loads `/admin/openapi.json` from the admin listener.
Get the machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 document served by this gateway process.
List caller API keys with plaintext credentials redacted.

post.description: Create a caller API key. The gateway validates the payload, rejects duplicate `key_hash` values, and returns the stored resource entry with plaintext credentials redacted.

requestBody.description: Caller API key configuration. Send the SHA-256 hash of the plaintext key. The Admin API stores only this hash and returns a plaintext credential only after key rotation.

400.description: Schema validation failed or the JSON body is malformed

413.description: JSON request body exceeds the admin body-size limit

415.description: Missing or unsupported JSON content type

delete.description: Delete a caller API key by ID.
delete.summary: Delete Caller API Key by ID

get.description: Get a caller API key by ID with plaintext credentials redacted.
get.summary: Get Caller API Key by ID

parameters.description: Caller API key resource ID generated by the Admin API.
parameters.name: id

put.description: Update a caller API key by ID. The gateway validates the payload, rejects duplicate `key_hash` values, preserves the resource ID, and increments the revision.

requestBody.description: Caller API key configuration. Send the SHA-256 hash of the plaintext key. The Admin API stores only this hash and returns a plaintext credential only after key rotation.

400.description: Schema validation failed or the JSON body is malformed

413.description: JSON request body exceeds the admin body-size limit

415.description: Missing or unsupported JSON content type

put.summary: Update Caller API Key by ID

parameters.description: Caller API key resource ID generated by the Admin API.
parameters.name: id

post.description: Rotate a caller API key by ID. The gateway generates a new plaintext key, stores only its SHA-256 hash, and returns the plaintext once.
post.summary: Rotate Caller API Key by ID

get.description: Get the liveness status of the admin process.

post.description: Forwards a chat completion through the proxy path for local playground testing. Use a proxy API key, not an admin key.

requestBody.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion request forwarded through the in-process proxy router.

schema.description: Server-sent events returned when the request body sets `stream` to `true`.

400.description: Malformed or invalid JSON request body.

401.description: Missing or invalid proxy API key.

403.description: Requested model is not allowed for the proxy API key or client IP.

413.description: Request body exceeds the proxy body-size limit.

429.description: Proxy rate limit or budget limit exceeded.

501.description: Proxy router is not wired into this admin router.

503.description: Proxy route unavailable or all routing targets filtered.
